rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert only into a valid owner scope for the annex block.
Use the authoritative model structure to match the correct owner kind and scope before merging content.
Keep component-type contract content with component types, and keep implementation-level bindings with implementations.
Do not introduce references that are invalid for the chosen owner scope, such as implementation-only paths in a type-owned annex.
generic_rules: Perform minimal structural edits: add the annex block without changing unrelated features, connections, properties, or hierarchy.
Maintain owner consistency so inserted content stays attached to the intended component or implementation declaration.
Preserve ordering and surrounding architecture text unless scope rules require placement after relevant declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope content belongs in an implementation owner and may need to appear after relevant subcomponents or connections.
anti_patterns: Do not move or rewrite unrelated architectural elements while fusing generated content.
Do not place type-scope contract content into an implementation owner or vice versa.
Do not rely on AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were in-scope AGREE variables during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: numeric range clause design details beyond owner/scope placement
